title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information about an evening cooking course.
audio_script: Woman: Good evening, everyone, and welcome to Northside College. I’m here to give you some information about the evening cooking course that begins next week. Your main teacher will be Mr Green, who runs the restaurant near the station. However, on the first evening you’ll meet our guest teacher, Anna [14]Mason[/14], who is famous locally for her bread and cakes. The course starts on [15]Tuesday[/15], not Monday as it says in the old leaflet, because the kitchen is being painted this weekend. Please arrive by six fifteen so we can begin at half past six. You don’t need to buy special clothes, but you must wear something [16]blue[/16] so the staff can easily recognise you in the building. If you have any questions before the course starts, call the college [17]office[/17] during the day. Please don’t contact the teachers directly, as they are usually busy preparing food. On the third week, we’ll visit the local [18]market[/18] to choose fresh vegetables, so bring a small bag with you then. Finally, there is a small charge for ingredients. It’s [19]five[/19] pounds each week, which you pay when you arrive. I think you’ll really enjoy learning to cook together. During the course, you will work in pairs, and the teacher will change the pairs each week so you can learn from different people. We’ll give you printed recipes, but you can also take pictures of the dishes after each class. At the end of the course, there will be a friendly meal for students and their families. Please tell us if you cannot eat anything for health reasons before the first lesson.

questions.explanation: The speaker says the guest teacher on the first evening is Anna Mason.
The speaker says the course starts on Tuesday, not Monday.
The speaker says students must wear something blue.
The speaker tells students to call the college office during the day.
The speaker says they will visit the local market in the third week.
The speaker says the ingredients cost five pounds each week.
